county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Urbana. Seat of early Swedenborgian (New Church) community; Urbana University records. Located in Congress Lands and partly Virginia Military District — check both survey systems. Mad River and Lake Erie Railroad records. Simon Kenton historical connections; early frontier settlement records. Champaign County Historical Society in Urbana. Early Shawnee presence — Treaty of Greene Ville (1795) ceded this area.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1805. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1805. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Champaign County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1805. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Champaign County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Champaign County Clerk of Courts, Urbana
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1805. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Champaign County Auditor, Urbana / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1805. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Champaign County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Springfield. Major industrial city; International Harvester, Crowell-Weed, and other factory records document worker families. Located partly in Virginia Military District and Congress Lands. Clark County Historical Society holds extensive local collections including city directories from 1850s. George Rogers Clark heritage. Springfield Race Riot of 1904 — newspaper records important. Wittenberg University archives. National Road corridor.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1817. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1817. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO

The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

source_profiles.repository: Clark County Clerk of Courts, Springfield / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Batavia. One of the earliest Ohio counties (1800). Located in the Virginia Military District — VA bounty land warrants essential for early land research. Ohio River county with steamboat and ferry records. Clermont County Genealogical Society maintains surname files and cemetery indexes. Grant's boyhood in Georgetown (Brown County) connects families. Significant early Quaker settlement records. Underground Railroad routes along the river.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1800. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Symmes Purchase Land Records — Clermont County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Cincinnati History Library and Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: John Cleves Symmes purchased approximately 1 million acres between the Great Miami and Little Miami Rivers. Records document original patent holders and early settlers in SW Ohio.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Ohio History Connection and Cincinnati History Library and Archives

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Clermont County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Wilmington. Major Quaker settlement — Wilmington Yearly Meeting records are extensive and well-preserved, available through Friends Historical Library. Clinton County Historical Society. Located partly in Virginia Military District. Wilmington College (Quaker, 1870) archives. DHL/Airborne Express hub history documented workers in later periods. Strong agricultural community — early plat maps and farm records at county historical society.

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Clinton County
source_profiles.repository: Wilmington College Quaker Heritage Center / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Quakers kept meticulous records. Ohio Yearly Meeting and Indiana Yearly Meeting records at Wilmington College.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed and indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Lisbon. Early Quaker settlement — Salem was a major anti-slavery center. Columbiana County was the birthplace of the Free Produce Movement and hosted anti-slavery conventions. Sandy and Beaver Canal records. Eastern Ohio industrial heritage — pottery and ceramics factory records (East Liverpool). Italian immigrant community in East Liverpool. Columbiana County Chapter OGS maintains research files. Check Jefferson County for pre-1803 records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Coshocton. Important Native American heritage — Coshocton was a major Delaware (Lenape) town. Roscoe Village (restored canal town) documents Ohio & Erie Canal era families. Canal construction worker records. Coshocton County Historical Society. Three Rivers area (Tuscarawas, Walhonding, Muskingum). Early Moravian missionary records. Johnson-Humrickhouse Museum holds local collections. Congress Lands survey.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1810. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bucyrus. Created 1820 from Delaware County. Located in Congress Lands. Colonel James Crawford (namesake) was burned at the stake in 1782 — pre-statehood frontier violence records. Strong German immigration; check Lutheran and Reformed church records. Crawford County Historical Society. Bucyrus was a railroad junction — railroad employment records. Agricultural county with township school records important.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Cleveland. Western Reserve county — major destination for Connecticut migrants. Cleveland is Ohio's largest city (historically); extensive city records, city directories (from 1837), and municipal archives. Western Reserve Historical Society (WRHS) is the primary research repository for NE Ohio — holds Connecticut Land Company records. Cuyahoga County Archives holds court, probate, and naturalization records from 1810. Massive immigration hub: German, Irish, Czech, Slovak, Polish, Hungarian, Slovenian, Italian, Jewish communities — ethnic church and fraternal organization records critical. Cleveland Public Library Special Collections. Case Western Reserve University archives.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1807. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Connecticut Western Reserve Land Records — Cuyahoga County
source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Greenville. Site of the Treaty of Greene Ville (1795) — treaty records at NARA and Ohio History Connection. Annie Oakley birthplace (North Star area). Located in Congress Lands. Strong German heritage — Versailles, Arcanum, and surrounding villages have extensive Catholic and Lutheran church records. Darke County Historical Society. Agricultural heartland — Darke is often Ohio's top agricultural county. Early Indian War records (1790s campaigns).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Defiance. Created 1845 from Williams, Henry, and Paulding. Fort Defiance built by General Anthony Wayne (1794) — military records at NARA. Confluence of Auglaize and Maumee Rivers. Miami & Erie Canal terminus area. Defiance College (1850) archives. Auglaize Village archaeological site. Strong German and Scots-Irish heritage. Check parent counties for pre-1845 records. Johnny Appleseed connection — nursery land records.
